input_text:
Tried using aioredis (async Redis client) inside Celery task handlers so we could share async code between our FastAPI app and our Celery workers.

The problem: Celery runs a synchronous event loop (it predates Python async). When aioredis tries to create an event loop inside a Celery worker, it conflicts with the loop Celery uses internally. Getting "There is no current event loop in thread" errors constantly.

Reverted to sync redis-py inside Celery workers. Kept aioredis only in the FastAPI async context where it belongs.

expected_negatives.what: Using aioredis (async Redis client) inside Celery task handlers
expected_negatives.why: Celery runs its own synchronous event loop; aioredis trying to create its own loop conflicted with Celery's, producing 'There is no current event loop in thread' errors. Reverted to sync redis-py inside Celery; kept aioredis only inside FastAPI's async context.

reviewer_notes: Tried, broke, reverted, with a clear runtime-conflict reason.
